$LLY  - GLP-1 MARKET TO HIT $150 BILLION BY 2030

TD COWEN RAISED ITS GLOBAL GLP-1 SALES FORECAST TO $150 BILLION BY 2030, UP FROM $139 BILLION, DRIVEN BY STRONGER DEMAND FOR DIABETES AND OBESITY TREATMENTS. THE FIRM NOW EXPECTS 59 MILLION PATIENTS ON GLP-1 THERAPIES BY 2030. ORAL WEIGHT-LOSS DRUGS ARE PROJECTED TO CAPTURE 14% OF SALES, WHILE ELI LILLY AND NOVO NORDISK ARE EXPECTED TO REMAIN DOMINANT WITH MARKET SHARES OF 62% AND 31%, RESPECTIVELY. ...

$TSLA  - TESLA'S FUTURE IS ROBOTICS, NOT CARS

J.P. MORGAN UPGRADED TESLA TO “NEUTRAL” FROM “UNDERWEIGHT,” SAYING THE STOCK’S VALUE IS INCREASINGLY TIED TO AUTONOMOUS DRIVING, ROBOTICS, AI, AND SOFTWARE RATHER THAN NEAR-TERM EV EARNINGS. THE BANK RAISED ITS PRICE TARGET TO $475 FROM $145, CITING TESLA’S STRONG HARDWARE-SOFTWARE INTEGRATION AND LONG-TERM GROWTH POTENTIAL. HOWEVER, IT WARNED THAT REGULATORY, SAFETY, AND EXECUTION RISKS REMAIN SIGNIFICANT. ...

MARKETS AT THEIR FROTHIEST SINCE 2008

CITI WARNS GLOBAL STOCKS ARE AT THEIR FROTHIEST LEVEL SINCE THE 2008 FINANCIAL CRISIS, WITH ITS BEAR MARKET CHECKLIST REACHING 10/18 RISK FLAGS GLOBALLY. RISING VALUATIONS, BULLISH SENTIMENT, AI-DRIVEN SPENDING, AND INCREASED IPO ACTIVITY ARE FUELING CONCERNS. WHILE CITI REMAINS POSITIVE ON EQUITIES THROUGH YEAR-END, IT CAUTIONS THAT IF RISK SIGNALS CONTINUE TO RISE, BUYING MARKET DIPS MAY BECOME INCREASINGLY RISKY. ...

🌅 Market News Digest
[Jun 3-4, 2026 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• U.S.-Iran tensions + Mideast ceasefire shifts — oil spiked near $96, risk assets fell; Trump said all-out war is unlikely unless U.S. troops are killed and talks on frozen Iranian funds are still unresolved
• Israel-Lebanon ceasefire in motion, but fragile — Washington brokered a deal/conditional truce, yet Hezbollah fire and Israeli strikes continued amid warnings the agreement hinges on withdrawals and disarmament
• Broadcom AI outlook shocks semis — shares plunged ~12%/$300bn+ erased after AI bookings/revenue guidance disappointed, hitting Asia tech and reviving AI-bubble worries
• Fed repricing turns hawkish — Logan and others flagged energy-driven inflation; markets now lean toward hikes by Dec 2026 instead of cuts

⛽ Oil & Energy
• Oil risk premium stays elevated — Novak said the market has not seen the full Mideast impact; Trump downplayed Hormuz mine risk and said routes could reopen if a ceasefire holds
• Russia/OPEC+ supply chatter — Novak said Russia may consider a diesel export ban, expects to meet quota, and OPEC+ won’t share UAE’s output quota
• Trump energy push — plans emerged for a $700M coal-plants/export initiative

📊 Markets & Macro
• Global equities soften, yields rise — U.S./Asia futures and major indices slid as higher oil pressured inflation expectations and Fed pricing
• Japan/Asia rates under pressure — BOJ seen likely hiking in June while later slowing bond-buying taper; JGB yields edged higher
• RBA stays hawkish on inflation — Bullock said inflation remains too high, policy is well positioned, and the labor-market/tradeoff is worsening
• Europe mixed data — eurozone retail sales and UK construction PMIs were weak; Sweden CPI surprised hotter, adding policy pressure

🌍 Geopolitical
• North Korea escalates nuclear rhetoric — Kim vowed to massively expand nuclear capabilities; South Korea said the facility is used for uranium enrichment
• Ukraine war remains active — Russia reported more drone attacks and casualties from a Ukrainian strike
• China-Taiwan tensions persist — Beijing condemned Taiwan/foreign lawmakers contacts and warned New Zealand visitors; Taiwan urged Beijing to stop interference

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Crypto selloff deepens — Bitcoin fell below $63k, longs saw large liquidations, and Strategy’s BTC drawdown hit about $11.5B unrealized
• ETH and majors weaken — Ether also slid as broad risk aversion and leveraged unwinds hit the market

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• AI regulation gains urgency — top AI executives urged Congress to tighten biosecurity checks on synthetic DNA/RNA purchases
• Trade tensions broaden — Australia and Malaysia pushed back on U.S. tariff/forced-labor issues; EU and China trade chiefs met to cool tensions
• BoJ/RBA policy guidance — Japan sources point to a June hike; Australia says rates are restrictive but inflation remains sticky

🏢 Corporate
• SpaceX eyes record IPO — reported plan to raise $75B, with Musk retaining about 82% voting power
• Mega-cap AI capex keeps flowing — Alphabet’s $85B raise and Meta’s $200/month AI assistant pricing show the AI monetization race continues
• TSMC stays constructive on AI demand — strong chip demand, U.S./Japan/Germany expansion, and High-NA EUV investment remain central themes
• Amazon expands Europe logistics — investing over €10B, adding 25,000 jobs and faster delivery capacity
